expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves donut-label assumptions for label visibility, density limits, and overlap-sensitive presentation
expectations.evidence: In result.md, the run states the required case focus but concludes it is not demonstrated: "The available context evidence does not contain explicit assumptions/requirements about: when donut slice labels are visible vs hidden; max slice/label count or density thresholds; collision/overlap rules" and gives the verdict "Not demonstrated / cannot be verified from provided evidence." This means the case focus is not covered by the produced outputs.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The output is explicitly scoped to Phase 1 and avoids later-phase work: result.md says "Phase under test: Phase 1 (context intake / spawn manifest generation)" and execution_notes.md states "This benchmark is Phase 1-focused; the result intentionally does not attempt Phase 3+ coverage mapping or drafting logic." The assessment is about Phase 1 artifacts (spawn manifest/context) being missing, which is phase1-aligned.
